findings.suggestion: Acquire c.mu in Get (Lock/Unlock around the read), or change mu to sync.RWMutex and wrap the read in RLock/RUnlock to keep reads concurrent while eliminating the race.
findings.summary: Cache embeds sync.Mutex (source.go:10) establishing the invariant that all access to c.data is lock-guarded; Set honors it (source.go:26-28) but Get reads c.data[key] with no lock (source.go:19-22). A read concurrent with Set's write is a data race under the Go memory model and can trigger the unrecoverable runtime `fatal error: concurrent map read and map write`, crashing the process. The comments on lines 18/20/24 acknowledge the bug but the mutex still gives false safety: the type advertises concurrency-safety it does not provide.
findings.title: Get reads shared map without holding mu — data race with Set

findings.suggestion: As part of the fix handoff, run `go mod init cache`, add a test that spawns concurrent Get/Set goroutines, and attach the output of `go test -race ./...` as the verification artifact before marking this resolved.
findings.summary: find over the repo shows only source.go — there is no go.mod and no *_test.go. The evidence gate rerun_relevant_verification is blocked: the change cannot be proven correct (or the race caught by `go test -race`/`go vet`) in the current tree. This is a fake-green risk — the fix described in the comments was never applied and nothing exercises the concurrent path.
findings.title: No verification possible: repo has no go.mod and no tests, so the race is undetectable by tooling

summary:
Risk-tiered review (lite) completed.

Single new file implementing a Cache. The implicit invariant 'all access to c.data is guarded by c.mu' is enforced in Set but violated in Get (source.go:20), which reads the map lock-free. This is a genuine data race that can cause an unrecoverable fatal runtime crash under the concurrent usage the code itself documents. The mutex creates a false impression of thread-safety. Fix by locking in Get (or using RWMutex).

Single new file implementing a Cache with a genuine, guaranteed data race: Get reads the map unsynchronized (line 20) while Set writes under the lock (lines 26-28). Under concurrent access this triggers Go's unrecoverable `concurrent map read and map write` fatal error, crashing the process and losing in-flight work — a real product-safety crash, not a style issue. Severity held at high rather than critical only because the type has 0 callers today (no live reproduction path); the defect itself is critical-grade and will fire the moment it's used concurrently as intended. Verification gate remains open: no race-detector run was performed.

Newly added cache/source.go contains a confirmed, self-documented data race: Get (source.go:19-22) reads the shared map without holding mu while Set (source.go:25-28) writes under the lock. In Go this is a process-fatal, unrecoverable runtime error, not a benign race. The struct comments acknowledge the bug but the fix (lock the reader) was never applied. Compounding the handoff risk, the repo has no go.mod and no tests, so the defect cannot currently be caught by `go test -race` — the verification gate remains blocked. Real blocker: synchronize Get. Optional-but-required-for-signoff: add module + race test and attach fresh output.
